title: Add search filter to dashboard and delete resolved incidents in admin panel

requirements.text: Add a search/filter input to the public-facing Dashboard that allows users to filter displayed incidents by keyword. The filter should match against incident title, affected system/service, and description fields. Both active and resolved incident sections should be filtered simultaneously. The filter input should be visible above the incident list and update results in real time as the user types. If no incidents match the search term, display a clear 'no results' message.

quotes: I would like a filter by search.

acceptanceCriteria: A search input field is visible on the Dashboard above the incident sections
Typing in the search field filters incidents in real time without a page reload
Filtering matches against incident title, affected system/service, and description
Both the active and resolved incident sections are filtered by the same search term
When no incidents match the search term, a 'no results' message is shown
When the search field is cleared, all incidents are shown again

requirements.text: Add a deleteIncident backend function to the Motoko actor that accepts an incident ID and removes the incident from the store. Only incidents with a 'resolved' status should be deletable; attempts to delete non-resolved incidents should return an error variant.

quotes: I would also like to delete resolved incidents if I need to.

acceptanceCriteria: A deleteIncident(id: Text) function exists in the backend actor
The function returns an ok result when a resolved incident is successfully deleted
The function returns an error result if the incident ID does not exist
The function returns an error result if the incident's status is not resolved
Calling getAllIncidents after deletion no longer returns the deleted incident

requirements.text: Add a delete button to each resolved incident in the Admin Panel. Clicking the button should call the deleteIncident backend function for that incident. After a successful deletion, the React Query cache for getAllIncidents must be invalidated so the admin panel and dashboard reflect the removal immediately. The delete action should require a confirmation step (e.g., a confirmation dialog or prompt) to prevent accidental deletions. The delete button should only appear for incidents with resolved status.

quotes: I would also like to delete resolved incidents if I need to.

acceptanceCriteria: A delete button is visible on resolved incidents in the Admin Panel
No delete button appears on non-resolved incidents
Clicking the delete button shows a confirmation prompt before proceeding
Confirming the deletion calls deleteIncident and removes the incident
After deletion, the incident list in the admin panel and dashboard is updated immediately
A useDeleteIncident mutation hook exists in useQueries.ts and invalidates the getAllIncidents cache on success

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Only resolved incidents may be deleted; the backend must enforce this constraint
The search filter must be client-side only and must not add new backend queries
nonGoals: Filtering or searching incidents from the Admin Panel
Bulk deletion of multiple incidents at once
Adding pagination or sorting controls to the dashboard
